speaker: PUCELLE
text: Dismay not, princes, at this accident,

speaker: PUCELLE
text: Nor grieve that Roan is so recoverèd.

speaker: PUCELLE
text: Care is no cure, but rather corrosive

speaker: PUCELLE
text: For things that are not to be remedied.

speaker: PUCELLE
text: Let frantic Talbot triumph for a while,

speaker: PUCELLE
text: And like a peacock sweep along his tail;

speaker: PUCELLE
text: We’ll pull his plumes and take away his train,

speaker: PUCELLE
text: If dauphin and the rest will be but ruled.

speaker: CHARLES
text: We have been guided by thee hitherto,

speaker: CHARLES
text: And of thy cunning had no diffidence.

speaker: CHARLES
text: One sudden foil shall never breed distrust.

speaker: BASTARD
text: Search out thy wit for secret policies,

speaker: BASTARD
text: And we will make thee famous through the world.

speaker: ALANSON
text: We’ll set thy statue in some holy place

speaker: ALANSON
text: And have thee reverenced like a blessèd saint.

speaker: ALANSON
text: Employ thee then, sweet virgin, for our good.

speaker: PUCELLE
text: Then thus it must be; this doth Joan devise:

speaker: PUCELLE
text: By fair persuasions mixed with sugared words

speaker: PUCELLE
text: We will entice the Duke of Burgundy

speaker: PUCELLE
text: To leave the Talbot and to follow us.

speaker: CHARLES
text: Ay, marry, sweeting, if we could do that,

speaker: CHARLES
text: France were no place for Henry’s warriors,

speaker: CHARLES
text: Nor should that nation boast it so with us,

speaker: CHARLES
text: But be extirpèd from our provinces.

speaker: ALANSON
text: Forever should they be expulsed from France,

speaker: ALANSON
text: And not have title of an earldom here.

speaker: PUCELLE
text: Your honors shall perceive how I will work

speaker: PUCELLE
text: To bring this matter to the wishèd end.

speaker: PUCELLE
text: Hark! By the sound of drum you may perceive

speaker: PUCELLE
text: Their powers are marching unto Paris-ward.

speaker: PUCELLE
text: There goes the Talbot with his colors spread,

speaker: PUCELLE
text: And all the troops of English after him.

speaker: PUCELLE
text: Now in the rearward comes the Duke and his.

speaker: PUCELLE
text: Fortune in favor makes him lag behind.

speaker: PUCELLE
text: Summon a parley; we will talk with him.

speaker: CHARLES
text: A parley with the Duke of Burgundy!

speaker: BURGUNDY
text: Who craves a parley with the Burgundy?

speaker: PUCELLE
text: The princely Charles of France, thy countryman.

speaker: BURGUNDY
text: What say’st thou, Charles?—for I am marching hence.

speaker: CHARLES
text: Speak, Pucelle, and enchant him with thy words.

speaker: PUCELLE
text: Brave Burgundy, undoubted hope of France,

speaker: PUCELLE
text: Stay; let thy humble handmaid speak to thee.

speaker: BURGUNDY
text: Speak on, but be not over-tedious.

speaker: PUCELLE
text: Look on thy country, look on fertile France,

speaker: PUCELLE
text: And see the cities and the towns defaced

speaker: PUCELLE
text: By wasting ruin of the cruel foe.

speaker: PUCELLE
text: As looks the mother on her lowly babe

speaker: PUCELLE
text: When death doth close his tender-dying eyes,

speaker: PUCELLE
text: See, see the pining malady of France:

speaker: PUCELLE
text: Behold the wounds, the most unnatural wounds,

speaker: PUCELLE
text: Which thou thyself hast given her woeful breast.

speaker: PUCELLE
text: O, turn thy edgèd sword another way;

speaker: PUCELLE
text: Strike those that hurt, and hurt not those that help.

speaker: PUCELLE
text: One drop of blood drawn from thy country’s bosom

speaker: PUCELLE
text: Should grieve thee more than streams of foreign gore.

speaker: PUCELLE
text: Return thee therefore with a flood of tears,

speaker: PUCELLE
text: And wash away thy country’s stainèd spots.

speaker: BURGUNDY
text: Either she hath bewitched me with her words,

speaker: BURGUNDY
text: Or nature makes me suddenly relent.

speaker: PUCELLE
text: Besides, all French and France exclaims on thee,

speaker: PUCELLE
text: Doubting thy birth and lawful progeny.

speaker: PUCELLE
text: Who join’st thou with but with a lordly nation

speaker: PUCELLE
text: That will not trust thee but for profit’s sake?

speaker: PUCELLE
text: When Talbot hath set footing once in France

speaker: PUCELLE
text: And fashioned thee that instrument of ill,

speaker: PUCELLE
text: Who then but English Henry will be lord,

speaker: PUCELLE
text: And thou be thrust out like a fugitive?

speaker: PUCELLE
text: Call we to mind, and mark but this for proof:

speaker: PUCELLE
text: Was not the Duke of Orleance thy foe?

speaker: PUCELLE
text: And was he not in England prisoner?

speaker: PUCELLE
text: But when they heard he was thine enemy,

speaker: PUCELLE
text: They set him free, without his ransom paid,

speaker: PUCELLE
text: In spite of Burgundy and all his friends.

speaker: PUCELLE
text: See then, thou fight’st against thy countrymen,

speaker: PUCELLE
text: And join’st with them will be thy slaughtermen.

speaker: PUCELLE
text: Come, come, return; return, thou wandering lord.

speaker: PUCELLE
text: Charles and the rest will take thee in their arms.

speaker: BURGUNDY
text: I am vanquishèd. These haughty words of hers

speaker: BURGUNDY
text: Have battered me like roaring cannon-shot,

speaker: BURGUNDY
text: And made me almost yield upon my knees.—

speaker: BURGUNDY
text: Forgive me, country, and sweet countrymen;

speaker: BURGUNDY
text: And, lords, accept this hearty kind embrace.

speaker: BURGUNDY
text: My forces and my power of men are yours.

speaker: BURGUNDY
text: So, farewell, Talbot. I’ll no longer trust thee.

speaker: PUCELLE
text: Done like a Frenchman: turn and turn again.

speaker: CHARLES
text: Welcome, brave duke. Thy friendship makes us fresh.

speaker: BASTARD
text: And doth beget new courage in our breasts.

speaker: ALANSON
text: Pucelle hath bravely played her part in this

speaker: ALANSON
text: And doth deserve a coronet of gold.

speaker: CHARLES
text: Now let us on, my lords, and join our powers,

speaker: CHARLES
text: And seek how we may prejudice the foe.
